body.label: Definition 1
body.definition:
**Definition 1 (Pseudo-length function).**
Let G be a group with identity element e. A function l : G -> R is a pseudo-length function on G if the following four conditions hold: nonnegativity, identity, symmetry, and triangle inequality.
body.name: PseudoLength

source.text:
**Definition 1 (Pseudo-length function).**
Let G be a group with identity element e. A function l : G -> R is a pseudo-length function on G if the following four conditions hold: nonnegativity, identity, symmetry, and triangle inequality.
source.label: Definition 1

body.text: The nonnegativity condition is included explicitly so that the codomain can be the usual real numbers in a formalization. Equivalently, one may take the codomain to be the set of nonnegative real numbers and omit the separate nonnegativity axiom.

body.label: Definition 2
body.definition:
**Definition 2 (Length function).**
Let G be a group with identity element e. A pseudo-length function l : G -> R is a length function if, for every g in G, g != e implies 0 < l(g).
body.name: IsLength

source.text:
**Definition 2 (Length function).**
Let G be a group with identity element e. A pseudo-length function l : G -> R is a length function if, for every g in G, g != e implies 0 < l(g).
source.label: Definition 2

body.label: Definition 3
body.definition:
**Definition 3 (Homogeneous pseudo-length function).**
Let G be a group. A pseudo-length function l : G -> R is homogeneous if, for every g in G and every integer n, l(g^n)=|n| l(g).
body.name: IsHomogeneousPseudoLength

source.text:
**Definition 3 (Homogeneous pseudo-length function).**
Let G be a group. A pseudo-length function l : G -> R is homogeneous if, for every g in G and every integer n, l(g^n)=|n| l(g).
source.label: Definition 3

body.text: In particular, for every g in G, homogeneity gives l(g^2)=2l(g).

An abelian group A is torsion-free if T(A)={0}.
body.name: IsTorsionFreeAbelianGroup

proof.text: Fix x,y in G. We first prove l(yxy^{-1}) <= l(x). Let n be a positive integer. By homogeneity, n l(yxy^{-1}) = l((yxy^{-1})^n). The group identity (yxy^{-1})^n = yx^n y^{-1} gives n l(yxy^{-1}) = l(yx^n y^{-1}). By the triangle inequality, l(yx^n y^{-1}) <= l(y) + l(x^n) + l(y^{-1}). By symmetry, l(y^{-1})=l(y). By homogeneity, l(x^n)=n l(x). Hence n l(yxy^{-1}) <= 2l(y)+n l(x). Dividing by n and letting n tend to infinity gives l(yxy^{-1}) <= l(x). For the reverse inequality, define z := yxy^{-1}. Then x=y^{-1}zy. Applying the inequality already proved with z in place of x and y^{-1} in place of y gives l(x) <= l(z)=l(yxy^{-1}). The two inequalities imply equality.

body.label: Theorem 14
body.name: theorem_14
body.claim: Let G be a group. Let l : G -> R be a homogeneous pseudo-length function. Then there exist a real Banach space B and a group homomorphism phi : G -> (B,+) such that l(g)=||phi(g)||_B for every g in G. Moreover, if l is a length function, then phi can be chosen to be injective.

proof.text: By Lemma 9, l factors through a homogeneous pseudo-length function barL : G_ab -> R. By Lemma 11, barL factors through the torsion-free quotient A:=G_ab/T(G_ab); let tildeL : A -> R be the induced homogeneous pseudo-length function. By Lemma 12, tildeL extends to a seminorm on VQ:=A tensor_Z Q. By Lemma 13, there exist a real Banach space B and a canonical seminorm-preserving map VQ -> B. Define phi:G -> B to be the composite G -> G_ab -> A -> VQ -> B. Each arrow is a group homomorphism, so phi is a group homomorphism, and by construction l(g)=||phi(g)||_B. If l is a length function and phi(g)=0, then l(g)=0, hence g=e. Thus phi is injective.
